This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Desmotrichum luxurians include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
